evidence_excerpt: At 18 months after resection of the meningioma, the patient remains independent in daily activities, and has had no further problems with her eyes.

quality_of_life.evidence_excerpt: No further problems with her eyes were reported at 18 months after surgery, and no ongoing symptoms related to the meningioma were described.

timeline_events.evidence_excerpt: At 18 months after resection, she has had no further problems with her eyes.

notes_on_uncertainty: Follow-up is reported only to 18 months; beyond that outcomes are unspecified. Functional status before surgery was already independent, so long-term improvement is inferred primarily from symptom resolution rather than functional change. Underlying Werner syndrome may affect baseline function, so functional limitation categorization is conservative.
